blurb_en:
Meta spent April collecting work samples from its employees — keystrokes, mouse clicks, and periodic screenshots from company-issued laptops — and feeding them into the company's AI training pipeline.
• The program was called the Model Capability Initiative (MCI), and Zuckerberg disclosed it himself at the April 30 all-hands meeting.
• MCI was reportedly being wound down when Zuckerberg revealed it — which is one way to get ahead of a story.
• Over 1,000 employees signed a petition demanding the program end. Meta did not respond to the petition.
• On May 20, Meta laid off roughly 7,800 people — the largest round since the 2022 year of efficiency.
• The timing is not lost on observers. No known causal link between MCI participation and who was let go.
• For companies with device telemetry programs: the line between product improvement data and AI training data is not obvious, and the people who draw it tend to work in legal.
blurb_zh:
Meta在四月份从员工的公司设备上采集“工作样本”——键盘击键、鼠标点击、以及定期截屏——并将这些数据输入公司的AI训练管道。
• 该项目名为“模型能力计划”（MCI），是Zuckerberg本人在4月30日全员大会上亲口披露的。
• MCI据报在Zuckerberg对外公开之际已开始收尾，这是一种很有意思的“提前告知”。
• 超过1000名员工联署请愿，要求立即终止该项目。Meta未作回应。
• 5月20日，Meta裁员约7800人，是2022年“效率之年”以来规模最大的一次。
• 时机巧合，有目共睹。目前尚无证据表明MCI参与程度与裁员名单存在直接关联。
• 对设有设备遥测项目的公司而言：产品优化数据与AI训练数据之间的界限并不显而易见，划这条线的人通常就职于法务部门。
verification_notes: Meta MCI program confirmed across Futurism, eWeek, IBTimes UK, Common Dreams. Zuckerberg disclosed at April 30 all-hands: program tracked keystrokes, clicks, screenshots on work laptops to train AI. ~7,800 layoffs on May 20 confirmed across major outlets. 1,000+ employee petition confirmed via Common Dreams and eWeek. All WebFetch 403s; verified via cross-referenced search snippets from 5+ sources.

blurb_en:
Starting this week, YouTube is letting any user insert themselves into another creator's Short using Gemini Omni — changing visual styles, adding new scenes, or generating an entirely new version — whether the original creator likes it or not.
• Announced at Google I/O May 19. Rolling out to YouTube Shorts Remix and the YouTube Create app.
• Creators can opt out — but the default is opt-in. Exclusion requires active consent from the original creator.
• All AI-remixed content is watermarked.
• Ask YouTube, also announced at I/O, gives YouTube Premium subscribers a conversational search interface — describe what you want to watch instead of typing keywords.
• This is the consumer distribution layer for Gemini Omni, not a new model.
• For camera companies and content creators: the line between remix and derivative work is now wherever YouTube draws it.
blurb_zh:
从本周起，任何YouTube用户都可以借助Gemini Omni将自己插入他人的短视频——改变画面风格、添加新场景，或生成全新版本——无论原作者是否情愿。
• 5月19日Google I/O宣布，推出至YouTube Shorts Remix功能及YouTube Create应用。
• 创作者可以选择退出——但默认是选择加入。想排除自己的内容，需要主动表态。
• 所有AI混剪内容均含水印标记。
• Ask YouTube同日公布：Premium用户可用对话方式描述想看什么，无需输入关键词搜索。
• 这是Gemini Omni的消费端分发落地，并非新模型。
• 对相机品牌和内容创作者而言：二次创作与衍生作品之间的边界，现在由YouTube划定。
verification_notes: Announced at Google I/O May 19. Confirmed via Android Central, Neowin, gHacks, shopifreaks (all citing YouTube official I/O announcement). Creator opt-out default opt-in and watermarking confirmed across sources. Ask YouTube Premium conversational search confirmed. Distinct from 2026-05-19-gemini-omni-io2026 (model launch capabilities); this covers the YouTube Shorts creator tools rollout.

blurb_en:
Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark told an Oxford audience on Wednesday that an AI will collaborate on a Nobel Prize-winning discovery within 12 months, AI-run companies will exist within 18 months, bipedal robots will be deployable in two years, and AI will be designing its own successors by 2028.
• He also said there is a non-zero probability that this ends with everyone dead.
• Clark described the current pace as inducing a 'vertiginous sense of progress' — the closest thing to a warning and an endorsement delivered in the same phrase.
• His stated biggest concern: not AI itself, but the speed at which institutions, regulators, and ordinary people are expected to adapt.
• Oxford produces many of the AI researchers currently building the systems he was describing.
• Twelve months from May 2026 is May 2027.
blurb_zh:
Anthropic联合创始人Jack Clark本周三在牛津演讲，预测：12个月内AI将参与诺贝尔奖级别的科学发现；18个月内出现由AI运营的公司；两年内双足机器人可商用部署；2028年前AI将开始设计自己的继任者。
• 他同时表示：这一切最终导致人类灭绝的概率，不是零。
• Clark用“令人眩晕的进步感”描述当前发展速度——同一句话既是赞美，也是警告。
• 他说最大的忧虑不是AI本身，而是机构、监管者和普通人被要求以多快速度适应。
• 牛津培养了许多正在构建这些系统的AI研究员。
• 2026年5月的12个月后，是2027年5月。
verification_notes: Jack Clark Oxford speech May 21 confirmed via TechCentral.ie, AIWeekly.co, resultsense.com, inshorts.com. Predictions consistent across all sources. Extinction risk quote confirmed. No primary transcript or recording verified — confidence medium.

blurb_en:
OpenAI launched a personal finance dashboard inside ChatGPT on May 15, letting Pro subscribers connect their bank accounts, investment portfolios, and subscriptions via Plaid's network of over 12,000 financial institutions.
• Exclusive to Pro users ($100/month). Free users are not invited to share their financial data.
• Dashboard surfaces portfolio performance, spending breakdowns, and active subscriptions inside a single conversation.
• Built on the Hiro team, a fintech startup OpenAI acquired in April 2026 for an undisclosed amount.
• In the same week, OpenAI also ran its self-serve Ads Manager rollout — collecting financial data on one end while monetizing attention on the other.
• For context: you now pay $100 per month to give ChatGPT a complete picture of where your money goes.
blurb_zh:
5月15日，OpenAI在ChatGPT内部上线个人财务仪表盘，允许Pro订阅用户通过Plaid连接逾12,000家金融机构的银行账户、投资组合和订阅服务。
• 仅限Pro用户（每月100美元）。免费用户不在共享财务数据的邀请名单内。
• 仪表盘在对话中汇总投资组合表现、消费明细和订阅追踪。
• 基于OpenAI今年4月以未披露金额收购的金融科技初创公司Hiro团队打造。
• 同一周，OpenAI还完成了自助广告投放平台的推出——一边收集用户财务数据，一边在另一端出售用户注意力。
• 换个说法：你每月花100美元，让ChatGPT全面掌握你的钱去了哪里。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch, 9to5Mac, MacRumors, American Banker, and OpenAI blog. Plaid integration, 12,000+ institutions, Pro-tier exclusivity, Hiro team acquisition (April 2026) all confirmed across sources. TechCrunch returns 403 on direct fetch; confirmed via search snippet cross-referencing from 5+ outlets.

blurb_en:
Judge William Orrick declined to approve the $1.5 billion Bartz v. Anthropic copyright settlement at the May 14 final approval hearing, sending both parties away with homework.
• The judge asked Anthropic and class counsel to submit a two-page supplemental brief by May 21 explaining the unusually high volume of late-filed opt-in claims.
• Opt-in rate climbed to 92.77% — roughly nine times the typical class action claim rate — with a meaningful number of authors apparently crossing the finish line after the deadline.
• Approval is expected 'relatively quickly' once the brief lands. The settlement itself is not in danger.
• If approved, it will be the largest copyright settlement in AI history — covering 482,460 books Anthropic used to train Claude without permission.
• The books, having been used, have already been used.
blurb_zh:
5月14日的最终批准听证会上，联邦法官William Orrick拒绝当庭批准价值15亿美元的Bartz诉Anthropic版权和解协议，而是给双方布置了作业。
• 法官要求Anthropic和集体诉讼代理律师在5月21日前提交不超过两页的补充简报，解释为何有大量索赔在截止日期后才提交。
• 整体索赔率已攀升至92.77%——约为普通集体诉讼索赔率的九倍——且显然有相当数量的作者在最后期限之后才赶到。
• 一旦补充简报提交，批准预计将“很快”到来。和解协议本身并无危险。
• 若获批准，这将是AI史上规模最大的版权和解——涵盖Anthropic在未获授权情况下用482,460部图书训练Claude的行为。
• 那些书，已经被用了。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Publishers Weekly and Authors Alliance post-hearing reports. Judge declined to approve at bench; asked for supplemental brief on late opt-outs due May 21; approval expected 'relatively quickly.' Opt-in rate 92.77% confirmed. Distinct from 2026-05-10-bartz-anthropic-settlement-final-hearing (pre-hearing status, 91.3% claim rate); this story covers the May 14 hearing outcome.
